description: This scenario tracks public support in South Korea for the IMF bailout package mandated during the 1997 Asian Financial Crisis. The intervention required strict austerity measures and structural reforms in exchange for a $58 billion rescue package to stabilize the crashing Won.

key_events.description: IMF signs the $58 billion bailout agreement requiring deep austerity.
National Gold Collecting Campaign begins, showing patriotic support for state economic recovery.
Major Chaebols begin layoffs, sparking intense union opposition and street protests.
South Korean presidential election outcome solidifies the commitment to IMF reform path.

calibration_notes: Useful for calibrating models on the tension between national sovereignty/austerity and international financial stabilization during sovereign debt crises.
